summary: Solid foundation with comprehensive unit tests for core cartesian product, include/exclude, and validation logic. Workflow structure validation is thorough (triggers, permissions, actionlint). However, critical gaps: (1) Only 2 act integration test cases provided (basic.json, include-exclude.json)—insufficient for 'every single test case must execute through act'; (2) CLI wrapper parameters (OutputFile, GitHubOutputName, SingleLine) lack explicit test coverage; (3) Edge cases sparse—no tests for type coercion consistency, axis ordering preservation, zero/negative max-parallel, null/empty axis values, special characters, or boundary conditions beyond 'at limit'; (4) act harness complexity with regex-based YAML parsing creates brittleness; (5) Only 2 fixtures means limited integration scenario coverage. Tests would catch obvious failures (cartesian product bugs, missing axes, oversize matrices) but risk missing subtle bugs in type handling, sorting, or parameter interactions. The requirement that 'all tests run through act' is partially met—unit tests and workflow tests run locally with Pester, not through act.
